Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log of insurance claims processing We need to identify cases with performance issues long durations analyze root causes based on attributes Resource Region Complexity and propose explanations and mitigations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First compute case durations from first event Submit Claim to last event Close Claim Lets extract times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2001 Submit 2024 04 01 09 00 Close 2024 04 01 10 30 duration 1 5 hours 90 minutes Very short

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2002 Submit 2024 04 01 09 05 Close 2024 04 02 11 00 from 09 05 to next day 11 00 is 25 hours 55 min so about 26 hours Thats longer than case 2001

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2003 Submit 2024 04 01 09 10 Close 2024 04 03 09 30 from 04 01 09 10 to 04 03 09 30 is 48h20m about 2 days 20 min

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2004 Submit 2024 04 01 09 20 Close 2024 04 01 10 45 1 hour 25 min 85 minutes Very short

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2005 Submit 2024 04 01 09 25 Close 2024 04 04 14 30 total 77h5m about 3 days 5h5m Thats very long

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So long duration cases 2002 about 26h 2003 about 48h and 2005 about 77h Case 2001 and 2004 are short under 2h

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now analyze attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Low complexity cases 2001 and 2004 are fast They have no Request Additional Documents steps They proceed straight from Evaluate to Approve

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Medium complexity 2002 has one Request Additional Documents step and takes about 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: High complexity 2003 and 2005 have multiple Request Additional Documents 2003 has two such steps and 2005 has three steps over multiple days So complexity level directly correlates with number of additional document requests which extends duration

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region Both A and B have fast low complexity cases But high complexity in Region A 2003 took about 2 days while high complexity in Region B 2005 took about 3 days

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Adjuster Mike handled 2001 low and 2003 high Adjuster Lisa handled 2002 medium 2004 low and 2005 high

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Possibly Adjuster Lisa tends to have more delays or the complexity of claims in Region B leads to more requests

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Manager Bill appears in both high complexity cases while Manager Ann appears in low and medium But the time between last request and approval is similar so approval time is not the main delay

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main delay is the multiple requests themselves especially the gaps between requests For 2005 there is a gap from 04 01 11 30 to 04 02 17 00 and then to 04 03 15 00

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be because Adjuster Lisa takes longer between requests or because the complexity in Region B requires more back and forth

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In 2002 request at 14 00 on 04 01 approval next day at 10 00 about 20h gap In 2003 last request at 17 00 approval next day at 16 00 about 23h gap So the main difference is the number of requests

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity is a clear driver high complexity claims require multiple additional document requests which increases duration significantly The number of requests and the time between them cause delays

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Region B might have more challenging claims or slower response from customers

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Adjuster Lisa might be less efficient or handle more complex claims but data is limited

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential root cause The adjusters efficiency or the complexity level determines how many times additional documents are needed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: No clear pattern for Finance Alan versus Finance Carl

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Mitigation suggestions For high complexity claims set clear timelines for each additional document request and follow up Consider assigning dedicated resources for complex claims Standardize the process for requesting documents to reduce delays Monitor adjuster performance and provide training if needed Implement a system to track document submission deadlines
